creators.name: Nick Saraev
creators.blurb: Practical AI automation and agency builds from someone shipping real systems.

videos.title: I Built a $1M/y SaaS with Claude Code, Here's How
videos.summary: Nick Saraev describes building Clairvo, a $1M/year SaaS that uses Claude Code-generated techniques like predictive pacing and local number rotation to improve call pickup rates. He argues that using Claude to enumerate opportunities and practical implementation of AI agents is viable for building real-world revenue-generating businesses, contrasting his approach with many who talk automation without results.

videos.title: How to Build Mobile Apps with Claude Code: Full Course (2026)
videos.summary: Nick Saraev's full-length course teaches mobile app development using Claude Code and Expo/React Native. The course covers setup, app design frameworks, building three complete apps (habit tracker, calorie tracker with AI, Pomodoro timer), database integration with Supabase, user authentication, and app store submission.

videos.title: How to Use Claude Code for FREE (2026)
videos.summary: Nick Saraev demonstrates how to use Claude Code for free or at minimal cost by routing through NVIDIA-NIM, OpenRouter, Ollama for local models, and alternatives like DeepSeek V4 Flash. The video includes setup instructions for each method and references a full 4-hour Claude Code course.

videos.title: Claude Opus-4.7 Just Dropped, And...
videos.summary: Claude Opus-4.7 is the latest version of Anthropic's Claude model. Nick Saraev discusses the release and its applications in a YouTube video covering practical automation workflows and business use cases.

creators.name: AI Engineer
creators.blurb: Talks and sessions from the AI Engineer conference and community.

videos.title: Why Can't Anyone Answer Questions About the Business? — Garrett Galow, WorkOS
videos.summary: Studio is an internal workspace that answers business questions in natural language against Snowflake, Linear, and Notion. It generates reusable declarative JavaScript widgets that call data sources directly, making subsequent runs deterministic and cheap. Three reliability techniques enable production use: preflight sequencing with just-in-time schema injection, explicit layering rules to force model reliance on primary sources over learned knowledge, and query validation before widget hardcoding.

videos.title: The agent-ready web: Simplify user actions with WebMCP — Tara Agyemang, Google
videos.summary: WebMCP is a proposed web standard from Google Chrome that enables AI agents to interact with websites through structured tools instead of DOM parsing and pixel-coordinate clicking. It provides both declarative HTML-based and imperative JavaScript APIs for defining agent-callable actions on web pages.

videos.title: How to Keep Shipping When You Walk Away from Your Desk — Zack Proser, WorkOS
videos.summary: Zack Proser argues that human attention is the hard constraint in agent-based development, not tool speed. He outlines a four-layer architecture: signal agents that monitor Slack and Linear, verification gates spanning lint to browser interaction, weekly self-improvement loops over conversation history, and health integration via Oura ring to maintain developer well-being during asynchronous agent execution.

videos.title: Stop Making Models Bigger, Make Them Behave — Kobie Crawdord, Snorkel
videos.summary: Snorkel finetuned a 4B model using RL to enforce tool discipline for database queries, enabling it to call get_table_name, inspect schema, and self-correct on errors. Training cost under $500 and outperformed Qwen 3 235B which hallucinated on the same task. Single-table training transferred to multi-table problems with improvements from 13.9% to 26.6% on FinQA benchmark.

creators.name: Cole Medin
creators.blurb: Practical AI agent and coding-assistant builds, end to end.

videos.title: Building with Anthropic's Fable 5 LIVE - Is It Really Worth the Hype?
videos.summary: Cole Medin tests Anthropic's Fable 5 model in a live stream to evaluate whether it justifies the hype and its 2x price premium over Claude Opus 4.8. The model is presented as Anthropic's most capable release to date.

videos.title: Google's Agents CLI: The CLI + Skills Combination to Ship AI Agents EASILY
videos.summary: Google has released an open-source Agents CLI that enables building and deploying production-grade AI agents with sandbox execution, audit trails, and identity management. The tool integrates with coding assistants like Claude and simplifies the agent development workflow from local testing through production deployment.

videos.title: Live AI Q&A + Crushing it in Chess at the Same Time - Come Hang Out!
videos.summary: Cole Medin is hosting a live AI Q&A stream on Saturday where viewers can ask questions about AI coding, agents, Claude Code, and Archon. The session will include chess gameplay alongside the discussion, positioning it as a casual community hangout event.

videos.title: Claude Plans, Gemini Designs: The Workflow to Build BEAUTIFUL Frontends
videos.summary: A workflow that composes Claude Opus for planning and reasoning tasks with Gemini 3.5 Flash for frontend design and implementation. The approach routes each phase to the model best suited for it: Opus handles specification and integration logic while Gemini generates UI code more efficiently and aesthetically.

creators.name: AI Jason
creators.blurb: AI app and agent builds, experiments, and walkthroughs.

videos.title: How to build proactive agents & self-improving company (Fully explained)
videos.summary: Video explains how to construct proactive AI agents and self-improving systems through autonomous loops. It covers building proactive AI loops and demonstrates use cases like autonomous ads optimization and SEO using tools like Loopany, memory systems, and agent tooling.

videos.title: Ralph-loop 2.0? The real autonomous coder is coming...
videos.summary: AI Jason discusses /goal feature and autonomous coding workflows including goal-buddy and mission exploration capabilities. The video covers setup, prompting strategies, and long-horizon task execution for next-generation autonomous coding agents.

videos.title: New AI coding paradiagm - OpenAI Symphony
videos.summary: OpenAI Symphony is presented as a new coding paradigm for AI development. The video demonstrates its setup, auto-testing capabilities, and workflow integration through a harness system.

videos.title: Okay, this unleashed my agent
videos.summary: A video overview comparing free AI agent frameworks and their capabilities. Topics include Autoresearch and AutoAgent for autonomous research, Claude's 3-layer memory system, Hermes and Openclaw self-evolution mechanisms, and the Crewlet multi-agent platform.

creators.name: Caleb Writes Code
creators.blurb: Hands-on coding sessions and software build-alongs.

videos.title: NVIDIA’s Nemotron 3 Is... Awesome?
videos.summary: NVIDIA released Nemotron 3 Nano, Super, and Ultra models. The series uses hybrid mamba transformer, MTP, and Latent MoE architectures to differentiate from other open models.

videos.title: MiniMax M3 explained in 8min..
videos.summary: MiniMax M3 is a large language model that introduces MiniMax Sparse Attention (MSA) to replace full attention mechanisms. The model includes optimizations for KV cache management, tiled I/O improvements, and continuous memory reading patterns that reduce latency during both prefill and decode inference stages.

videos.title: Pi Agent explained in 6min..
videos.summary: Pi is an agent framework that includes a harness layer for controlling agent behavior. The video compares Pi against other frontier coding agents like Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, and Antigravity, discussing how Pi's harness architecture handles increasing product complexity.

videos.title: Huawei makes 1.4nm chip??
videos.summary: Caleb Eom discusses Huawei's announcement of Tau Scaling Law as part of the China-USA semiconductor race. The video covers how EUV and DUV lithography, packaging, and logic folding contribute to advancing chip manufacturing capabilities amid growing geopolitical competition.

creators.name: AWS Developers
creators.blurb: Official AWS tutorials, service deep dives, and developer demos.

videos.title: AI and the Job Market in 2026
videos.summary: AWS Developers discusses strategies for tech job hunting in 2026 as AI screening becomes common. The video argues that junior developers remain valuable, personal branding and networking outperform mass applications, and developers should build projects and contribute to open source to stand out.

videos.title: 5 Tips for Deploying AI Agents to Production
videos.summary: AWS Developers presents five production deployment best practices for AI agents: streaming tool events for UI responsiveness, layered authentication with OAuth and IAM, contracted database access with tenant isolation, guardrails with cost budgets and model routing, and observability with metrics and distributed tracing. Patterns are demonstrated using TypeScript with Strands Agents SDK and AWS services but claimed to apply generally.

videos.title: Infrastructure as Code for Beginners (AWS CDK)
videos.summary: A YouTube tutorial from AWS Developers on Infrastructure as Code fundamentals using AWS CDK with Python. The tutorial demonstrates building a serverless REST API with DynamoDB, Lambda, and API Gateway, comparing manual console setup against CDK-based Infrastructure as Code.

videos.title: Stop AI Hallucinations With These 5 Techniques
videos.summary: AWS Developers presents five techniques to reduce AI hallucinations in agents: semantic tool selection to filter context, graph RAG for precise queries, multi-agent validation to catch failures, symbolic guardrails implemented in code, and runtime steering for self-correction. Demonstrates each technique with a travel agent example and shows how Amazon Bedrock AgentCore implements these at scale.

creators.name: Visual AI
creators.blurb: Visual walkthroughs and demos across modern AI tools.

creators.name: Alberta Tech
creators.blurb: Developer tutorials and practical builds in AI and software.

videos.title: Do Google engineers actually vibe code?
videos.summary: Alberta Tech discusses whether Google engineers use AI code generation ("vibing code") and compares vibing practices at Google with OpenAI Codex and Claude Code. The video covers current and predicted future trends in AI-assisted coding at Google through 2026.

videos.title: This company killed open source
videos.summary: Alberta Tech discusses Malus, a company and tool that the creator frames as disruptive to open source maintenance practices. The video explores tensions between AI agents, open source sustainability, and the future of maintainer incentives in response to commercial pressure.

videos.title: I let Claude Code take my job (it did better than me)
videos.summary: Alberta Tech conducted an experiment comparing Claude Code against their own coding job performance. The creator tests whether Claude Code can outperform human developers and explores integration with Higgsfield's model context protocol tooling.

creators.name: EO
creators.blurb: Conversations and stories with founders and builders in tech.

videos.title: How to Steal Customers From Giants 600x Bigger than Your Startup | Pigment, Eléonore Crespo
videos.summary: Eléonore Crespo, co-founder of Pigment, discusses how her company competes with much larger incumbents by building a full enterprise platform for performance management that integrates finance, supply chain, HR, and sales data. She argues that building the hard path of a complete platform from day one, rather than shipping single features, creates a sustainable competitive advantage, and emphasizes that hiring discipline and securing endorsements from respected companies are critical to enterprise growth.

videos.title: The Fastest Way to Know if Your Product Market Fit Is Real | Serval CEO, Jake Stauch
videos.summary: Jake Stauch, CEO of Serval, argues that real product-market fit manifests as customer pull rather than push, and that embedded customer relationships reveal true demand better than formal interviews. He warns that passionate early adopters often mask weak market fit and advocates for betting on where AI models are heading rather than their current capabilities.

videos.title: He Raised $26M Betting on Probabilities, Not Guesses | Koah Labs, Nic Baird
videos.summary: Nic Baird founded Koah Labs, an ad monetization platform for AI tools, and raised $26M by betting on advertising as the primary business model for AI applications. The video discusses his decision-making framework from competitive sailing, why subscriptions have failed for AI apps, and identifies human trust adoption rather than technology as the bottleneck for AI agents.

videos.title: I Hit $1M ARR in 117 Days. Bootstrapped to $10M. Here's My Playbook | Chatbase, Yasser Elsaid
videos.summary: Yasser Elsaid, founder of Chatbase, shares his bootstrapped growth playbook from $0 to $10M ARR in the span of 117 days to $1M and beyond. He covers strategies for reducing churn, product-led growth, SEO optimization, pricing experiments, and decision-making frameworks for early-stage founders.

creators.name: Cyril Imhof
creators.blurb: AI automation and workflow builds for real projects.

videos.title: The Ultimate Second Brain for AI Entrepreneurs: Fable 5 Claude with Loops
videos.summary: Cyril Imhof discusses Fable 5, an Anthropic model designed for AI entrepreneurs, highlighting its features, guardrails, memory capabilities, and agentic payment integration compared to Mythos 5.

videos.title: Anthropic's Brand New AI Just Started the Gold Rush (Fable 5)
videos.summary: Cyril Imhof discusses Anthropic's new Claude Fable 5 model and its applications in regulated industries. The video covers new use cases for the model and introduces "Know Your Agent" methodology for effective context engineering in long-running AI applications.

creators.name: PY
creators.blurb: Software engineering, tooling, and developer-workflow deep dives.
